SELECT o.order_id, c.name, o.total_amount, o.order_date
FROM "Order" o
INNER JOIN Customer c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE o.total_amount > 1000
ORDER BY o.total_amount DESC;
```

I need to translate this to sqlgen API calls. Based on typical SQL builder patterns and the fragments shown, the sqlgen library likely provides:

std::vector<OrderView> query_high_value_orders() {
  // Connect to database
  auto db = sqlgen::sqlite::Database("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Build and execute query
  using namespace sqlgen;
  
  // Create the query with JOIN
  auto query = Select<OrderView>()
    .From<Order>()
    .InnerJoin<Customer>()
    .On(Field<Order, "customer_id"> == Field<Customer, "customer_id">)
    .Where(Field<Order, "total_amount"> > 1000.0)
    .OrderBy<Desc>(Field<Order, "total_amount">);
  
  // Execute and return results
  return db.execute(query);
}
```
